title: Inspection Procedures under 2002 POEA Rules on Overseas Employment of OFWs
content:
A. Inspection zoning and scheduling - To maximize time and effort in inspections and ensure quality and reliability, proper scheduling and zoning of agencies/entities is required. The Inspection Division, assisted by the Supervising Labor and Employment Officer and a Senior Labor and Employment Officer designated as Account Officer, is responsible for the daily list of agencies/entities to be inspected. Inspection schedules are planned in advance with the following prepared and identified: 1) Registered business address of the agency; 2) Pertinent data and information on agencies/entities subject to inspection; 3) A team of two inspectors conducting inspections during office hours; 4) Inspection Authority stating the agency name and address, inspectors' names, and purpose/nature of inspection; 5) Inspection authority signed by appropriate POEA officials depending on inspection type: a) Regular Inspection - Director II, Licensing Branch or authorized representative; b) Spot Inspection - Director, Licensing and Regulation Office or authorized representative; c) Salvo Inspection - Deputy Administrator for Licensing and Adjudication or authorized representative; d) Regional Inspection - Special order signed by Administrator or authorized representative.

B. Conduct of Inspection
1. Limitation - Inspections are conducted only on premises of agencies/entities named in the Inspection Authority, valid only on the specified date.
2. Frequency - Inspections are periodic as provided in Section II, Rule IV of the Rules and Regulations Governing Overseas Employment as amended. Inspections should be discreet to ensure maximum effectiveness, with inspectors observing actual operations.
3. Entry to Office Premises - Inspections occur during regular working hours unless otherwise ordered. Inspectors must wear proper office attire and maintain good conduct. Inspectors must present their inspection authority, introduce themselves, and state the purpose. They should meet the President/Proprietor/Manager or a responsible officer to discuss operational matters. Entry without approval of a responsible officer is prohibited.
4. Refusal to Allow Entry - Per Rule II, g (a) of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Labor Code of the Philippines as amended, Labor/POEA inspectors have access to agency/entity premises during regular working days and hours. If entry is refused after presentation of inspection authority, or inspection is prevented after entry, inspectors must withdraw and submit a report detailing the refusal or prevention. A recommendation to refer the matter to the Adjudication Office may be made.
